title: vellumwidget — agent notes
text: vellumwidget turns compiled vellumplot scene interactive htmlwidget. host adapter: consumes already-compiled, static vellum scene (SVG data-keys + scene_model() element table) layers interaction top browser. compile grammar.

what: Interactivity: prefer the spec over new as_widget() flags
title: vellumwidget — agent notes
text: as_widget() already carries ~36 arguments, behaviour flags (hover, select, brush, lasso, legend_click, crosshair, hover_mode, navigator, axis_zoom, …). adding another interaction/behaviour argument, stop consider whether behaviour belongs vellumplot spec instead — declared part plot — rather one imperative widget flag. matters: widget flag per-plot, per-host, imperative config, low ceiling. express cross-view coordination (brush panel → filter panel B), portable host, travel plot spec saved / printed / round-tripped. vellumplot spec--data grammar. Interaction declared spec — named selection referenced conditional encoding, filter, scale domain (Vega-Lite’s model) — portable, composable, serialisable. intended long-term home interaction: “declarative interactivity” direction (Phase 6 vellumplot Tier-2 plan). built yet, building deliberate, checkpointed design decision — every new as_widget() flag added meantime one thing future declarative model duplicate migrate. route interaction spec, keep display-tier. vellumplot compile-design deliberately reactive dataflow runtime. spec-declared interactions must reactions host can perform frozen scene — highlight/dim, hide/isolate, filter--hiding, pan/zoom, linked brushing. Interactions recompute grammar (re-bin re-aggregate brush, retrain scale filtered data) scope client-side; need Shiny recompile. Rule thumb. new as_widget() behaviour fine genuinely widget-local, single-plot affordance. smells like “plot know ,” needs coordinate two views, route spec flag maintainer. tripwire test tests/testthat/test--widget.R fails as_widget() argument count grows past current baseline — purpose force exactly consideration (remind Phase 6 direction) signature grows .

title: Very large scenes
text: raster_threshold keyed elements (default 20000), as_widget(mode = "auto") — default — renders scene single embedded image drives interaction compact element index rather DOM. scatter 30,000 keyed points, "auto" chose raster path. image base64 PNG wrapped SVG shell whose viewBox scene’s device-pixel space, pan zoom reframe image viewBox element bounding boxes line without rescaling. Hover, click, brush hit-test index keys boxes, keep working even though nothing per-point DOM. can force strategy instead letting "auto" decide: Use "svg" want per-element behaviour (grammar colours, screen-reader focus, filter culling) plot near threshold can afford nodes. Use "raster" opt smaller plot image path, lift raster_threshold keep SVG path longer.
code: library(vellumplot)  set.seed(1) n <- 30000 big <- data.frame(   x = rnorm(n),   y = rnorm(n),   id = seq_len(n) ) vplot(big) |>   mark_point(x = x, y = y, data_id = id, size = 1) |>   as_widget() as_widget(p, mode = "svg")    # always per-element SVG as_widget(p, mode = "raster") # always the single image as_widget(p, mode = "auto", raster_threshold = 50000) # move the switch point

title: Very large scenes
text: Three changes carry load, apply whether plot ends SVG raster. Columnar payload. keyed-element metadata serialised one array per field (keys, x-coordinates, ) instead one JSON object per element. Serialising N tiny objects N allocations R side dominated build large N. 150,000-point scatter, building serialising payload dropped 24 s / 89 MB 0.4 s / 12 MB. widget reconstructs per-element view browser side, pure wire-format change behavioural difference. Spatial index. Nearest-mark hover rectangular brush hit-test Flatbush R-tree rather scanning every element. nearest-mark scan runs every pointer move, replacing O(n) scan O(log n) query change feel dense plot. Cheaper hover dim. threshold, hovering dims whole plot container’s opacity redraws just hovered marks crisply small overlay — O(hovered) — instead restyling every element CSS, forced full-scene style recalc hover. Small moderate plots keep exact per-mark dim .

title: Very large scenes
text: Scaling raster blurs . zoom raster-mode plot, widget redraws points view sharply <canvas> overlay: samples point’s colour straight rendered image takes position size element index, sharp layer matches vellum drew. overlay engages zoomed — full view see faithful, anti-aliased base image — redraws points currently screen. 2D canvas context unavailable falls back scaled image alone.

title: Very large scenes
text: Raster mode buys scale giving per-element DOM, things need per-element nodes apply: Per-element grammar colours (mark’s hover_color / selected_color) node style. widget-wide hover_color / selected_color / dim_opacity still work. Per-mark screen-reader focus gone — focusable graphics-symbol nodes tab . chart keeps accessible name description (scene title/alt text), per-mark traversal. keyboard/screen-reader navigation individual marks matters scale given plot, force mode = "svg". Display-tier cross-filtering (crosstalk filter_* inputs, vw_filter()) nodes hide, cull raster plot. Crosstalk selection still round-trips key. zoomed-view scaled raster refined crisp-zoom canvas, re-rendered scene, reveal detail finer base image. Everything else unchanged: hover tooltips highlight, click brush selection, pan zoom, group-linked crosstalk selection (keyed), toolbar, export. Small moderate plots never touch path behave exactly .

what: The one thing that has to match: the key
title: Linking views with crosstalk
text: crosstalk coordinates widgets shared key. Every row data carries key, widget reports receives selections terms keys. vellumwidget key mark’s data_id. rule simple: SharedData key data_id must column. SharedData$new(df, key = ~model) wraps data declares model key. Build plot sd$origData() (wrapped frame), set data_id = model mark widget’s keys line key column, pass SharedData as_widget(crosstalk =): plot draws data, crosstalk = sd tells widget group keys coordinate .
code: library(vellumplot) library(crosstalk)  df <- data.frame(   wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, hp = mtcars$hp,   model = rownames(mtcars), cyl = factor(mtcars$cyl) ) sd <- SharedData$new(df, key = ~model) scatter <- vplot(sd$origData()) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = cyl, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget(crosstalk = sd, width = 360, height = 300)

title: Linking views with crosstalk
text: Put scatter next DT table built SharedData selection travels ways: click brush points plot matching rows highlight table; select rows table points light . Nothing snippet wires two together explicitly. agree share sd, sd carries keys. hood vellumwidget keeps selection engine registers crosstalk SelectionHandle, click plot sets crosstalk selection (table reads) crosstalk selection table drives widget’s highlight. Selection still projects hover_group vellumwidget side, marks declare one, selecting single point can select whole series series crosstalk broadcasts.
code: crosstalk::bscols(   scatter,   DT::datatable(sd, rownames = FALSE, options = list(pageLength = 6)) )

title: Linking views with crosstalk
text: crosstalk’s filter_* inputs second, independent channel. filter narrows set visible keys; every widget bound group shows drops rest. vellumwidget side filter dims hides non-matching marks (display-tier cross-filter, mechanism vw_filter() uses Shiny) without touching data behind . Selection filtering compose: filter restricts screen, selection highlights within remains.
code: crosstalk::bscols(   widths = c(3, 9),   list(     filter_checkbox("cyl", "Cylinders", sd, ~cyl),     filter_slider("hp", "Horsepower", sd, ~hp)   ),   vplot(sd$origData()) |>     mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = cyl, data_id = model) |>     as_widget(crosstalk = sd, width = 420, height = 320) )

title: Linking views with crosstalk
text: crosstalk-aware widget joins group. leaflet map keyed way links markers scatter: plotly works way — build plotly trace shared data coordinates widget:
code: sd_geo <- SharedData$new(cities, key = ~id) crosstalk::bscols(   leaflet::leaflet(sd_geo) |> leaflet::addTiles() |> leaflet::addCircleMarkers(),   vplot(sd_geo$origData()) |>     mark_point(x = lon, y = lat, data_id = id) |>     as_widget(crosstalk = sd_geo) ) sd <- SharedData$new(df, key = ~model) crosstalk::bscols(   plotly::plot_ly(sd, x = ~wt, y = ~mpg, text = ~model),   vplot(sd$origData()) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |> as_widget(crosstalk = sd) )

title: Linking views with crosstalk
text: vellumwidget three linking directions, answer different questions. native group bus links vellumwidget widgets dependency (see interactive tour). Reach every linked view vellumwidget. crosstalk links vellumwidget widget rest htmlwidgets ecosystem, client-side, still Shiny. Reach moment DT, plotly, leaflet view, filter_* control, picture. Shiny inputs vellumwidget_proxy() server needs see drive selection. See Shiny article. crosstalk Suggests. client library bundled actually pass SharedData (group name) crosstalk =; plain widget carries crosstalk code. group-linked widget crosstalk-linked widget can coexist document — use separate buses. One caveat raster-mode widgets (see large scenes): crosstalk selection still round-trips key, display-tier filter per-element nodes hide, filter_* inputs cull raster plot.

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Map tooltip data_id hover point: shows tooltip dims others. data_id join key; tooltip box says.
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = cyl, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget()

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text:
Tooltip text rendered safe HTML: build multi-line, formatted string tooltip = (glue) using
. Data values escaped, inert tags (, <>,
, ) honoured, injection risk. Style box tooltip_style. Tune behaviour : tooltip_delay waits beat showing (calmer dense scatter), tooltip_follow = FALSE anchors mark instead tracking cursor, tooltip_sticky = TRUE lets move tooltip — useful HTML holds link button. box auto-flips cursor near top edge stays within widget.
code:
df$label <- glue::glue("{df$model}
{df$mpg} mpg · {df$wt}k lbs") vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = label, data_id = model) |> as_widget(tooltip_style = list(background = "#1d3557", fontsize = "13px"))

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: single scatter, hovering nearest point want. multi-series line time-series chart usually want opposite: every series’ value cursor’s x, one box. Set hover_mode = "x" unified hover — every mark sharing hovered x highlights together tooltip joins one combined box. "y" along y; "closest" (default) keeps single-nearest behaviour. Turn crosshair = TRUE drop guide rule hovered position (vertical "x" mode, horizontal "y", full cross "closest"). Unified hover reads mark positions straight element index — axis metadata needed — works keyed plot raster mode. snaps along axis, shared readout tracks cursor anywhere plot. Shiny, input$<id>_hover still reports single nearest mark.
code: long <- data.frame(   t = rep(1:12, 2),   y = c(cumsum(rnorm(12, 1)), cumsum(rnorm(12, 0.5))),   series = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 12) ) vplot(long) |>   mark_line(x = t, y = y, color = series) |>   mark_point(x = t, y = y, color = series, tooltip = y, data_id = interaction(series, t)) |>   as_widget(hover_mode = "x", crosshair = TRUE)

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Click mark select (every mark sharing data_id toggles together); drag rectangle brush-select. select_mode = "single" makes click replace selection instead toggling. also freehand lasso (default) — switch toolbar’s mode button (cycles brush → lasso → pan) drag loop; every mark whose centre falls inside selected. Turn lasso = FALSE. works every keyed mark, just points. statistical mark draws several shapes share one key — error bar bar plus two caps, boxplot box rectangle plus median whiskers — hovering, selecting, brushing part lights toggles whole mark unit (boxplot’s outliers stay individually addressable). Declare data_id/tooltip mark_errorbar(), mark_linerange(), mark_boxplot() way.
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model, selected_color = "#e63946") |>   as_widget() df2 <- data.frame(g = c("A", "B", "C"), y = c(20, 26, 23), lo = c(17, 24, 21), hi = c(23, 28, 25)) vplot(df2) |>   mark_errorbar(x = g, ymin = lo, ymax = hi, data_id = g, tooltip = g) |>   as_widget()

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: discrete color shape scale draws interactive legend automatically: hover swatch highlight series, click select . Set legend_click = "hide" make legend visibility toggle instead — single click hides shows series, double-click isolates (hides every series; double-click bring back). reflexive legend interaction plotly friends. "mute" dims series rather removing , keeping layout steady. Hovering swatch still highlights series every policy, hidden series drops hover tooltip hit-testing. works keyboard (Tab swatch, Enter/Space toggle) independent crosstalk cross-filter.
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = cyl, data_id = model) |>   as_widget(legend_click = "hide")

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: plot maps continuous color scale, colorbar becomes interactive filter: drag two handles value range marks outside fade (stop responding hover/brush); double-click bar reset. Shiny range arrives input$<id>_colorfilter. ’s automatic — argument — whenever ’s continuous colorbar.
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = mpg, data_id = model) |>   as_widget()

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Zoom mouse wheel, drag pan (toolbar’s mode button cycles brush → lasso → pan). touch device, drag pans two-finger pinch zooms; widget focused, arrow keys pan, +/- zoom, 0 resets. -hover toolbar adds zoom--selection, reset, SVG/PNG download, copy--clipboard (supported), fullscreen.

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Zoom scales plot’s data region re-ticks axes visible range, holding frame — axes, titles, legend — place, way charting library zooms (rather scaling whole scene like image, tick labels grow blur). Hover, brush, crosshair, reported data range follow zoomed region. default: applies single linear cartesian panel (continuous identity/reverse axes) SVG mode. Plots log/date/discrete axes, several panels, raster mode silently fall back whole-scene zoom, always safe leave ; pass axis_zoom = FALSE force plain whole-scene zoom. Glyph marks (points, circles, hexagons) keep constant pixel size zoom — positions re-map, points stay round never stretch ellipses. Bars, error bars, lines still scale data (extent data); stroke width held constant. Pass zoom_marks = "scale" let points grow zoom instead (useful read density dense scatters).
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = model) |>   as_widget()

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: long series, navigator = TRUE adds strip plot showing whole scene miniature, draggable, resizable window marking visible x-range. Drag window pan, drag handle zoom — stays sync main view (wheel, keyboard, brush, linked-group changes move ). navigator x-range tool, zoom one-dimensional: selected x-range fills width full y-range stays screen (values never scroll top bottom), way time-series range selector behaves. axis-aware zoom (default), x-axis re-ticks crisply scrub; plot falls back whole-scene zoom view stretches horizontally instead (point marks scale anisotropically) — navigator aimed line/area/series charts. Export captures current view, zoomed-region exports shown. Set download name hi-res PNG scale:
code: long <- data.frame(t = 1:200, y = cumsum(rnorm(200))) vplot(long) |>   mark_line(x = t, y = y) |>   as_widget(navigator = TRUE) vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |>   as_widget(export_filename = "mtcars", export_scale = 2)

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Accessibility default (a11y = TRUE). widget announces interactive chart, every mark focusable: Tab chart, use arrow keys move marks (announced polite live region), Enter/Space select, Escape leave traversal mode. visually-hidden data table lists every mark screen-reader users. chart’s accessible name description come plot’s title alt text (vellumplot sets automatically) explicit as_widget(alt =). See vellumplot Accessibility article full cross-package story (alt text, accessible SVG/PDF, widget).

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Widgets sharing group link client-side: selecting brushing one highlights data keys others, panning zooming one moves others relative view — Shiny crosstalk. Selection projects hover_group marks declare . Linked pan/zoom shares view fraction widget’s extent, stays aligned even linked plots different sizes (small multiples). Try zooming one — follows. interop plotly / leaflet / DT crosstalk’s filter_* controls, pass crosstalk::SharedData (group name) crosstalk = instead — see Linking views crosstalk.
code: p <- vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model, hover_group = cyl) htmltools::tagList(   as_widget(p, group = "cars", width = 320, height = 240),   as_widget(vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model, hover_group = cyl),             group = "cars", width = 320, height = 240) )

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: hover_color, selected_color, dim_opacity set widget-wide theme; per-mark hover_color / selected_color declared vellumplot override mark. tooltip_style themes tooltip box ().
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |>   as_widget(hover_color = "seagreen", selected_color = "firebrick", dim_opacity = 0.15)

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: vellumwidgetOutput() / renderVellumwidget() embed widget Shiny app, widget reports user’s selection, clicks, hovers, brush, current view back server reactive inputs. vellumplot plot brush view also report data-space coordinates (just pixels) — e.g. input$plot_brush$x0d/x1d input$plot_zoom$data$x — server can act data ranges directly. See Shiny article ?vellumwidget-shiny.

title: Using vellumwidget in Shiny
text: Render widget standard htmlwidgets pair, exactly like plotly DT:
code: library(shiny) library(vellumplot) library(vellumwidget)  ui <- fluidPage(   vellumwidgetOutput("plot", height = "500px"),   verbatimTextOutput("info") )  server <- function(input, output) {   output$plot <- renderVellumwidget({     vplot(mtcars) |>       mark_point(         x = wt, y = mpg,         tooltip = rownames(mtcars),         data_id = rownames(mtcars) # <- the key each interaction reports       ) |>       as_widget()   })    output$info <- renderPrint({     input$plot_selected # the keys currently selected   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using vellumwidget in Shiny
text: inputs named <outputId>_<event>, vellumwidgetOutput("plot") input$plot_selected, input$plot_click, input$plot_hover, input$plot_brush, input$plot_zoom. Every selection value expressed element keys (data_id set mark), map back data key. Data-space fields (x0d/… zoom$data) appear plot carries cartesian scale (vellumplot plot); raw vellum scene reports device-pixel fields . describe visual axes — x0d/x1d horizontal axis, coord_flip() plot’s y aesthetic. Date/time axes report numeric epoch (days Date, seconds POSIXct) — map back .Date() / .POSIXct(). discrete axis reports fractional band positions, axis custom scales::transform_*() (beyond identity / log10 / sqrt / reverse) omitted data-space fields. State vs event. State inputs (_selected, _hover, _zoom) update value changes, re-selecting set (settling view) re-fire; pair observe() / reactive(). Event inputs (_click, _brush) fire every occurrence, even repeat; pair observeEvent().

title: Using vellumwidget in Shiny
text: everything keyed, common patterns one-liners. Filter table brushed points: Show hovered car: Respond click:
code: output$table <- renderTable({   keys <- input$plot_brush$keys   if (length(keys)) mtcars[rownames(mtcars) %in% keys, ] else mtcars[0, ] }) output$detail <- renderText({   k <- input$plot_hover   if (is.null(k)) "Hover a point" else paste(k, "-", mtcars[k, "hp"], "hp") }) observeEvent(input$plot_click, {   key <- input$plot_click$key   if (!is.null(key)) showNotification(paste("clicked", key)) })

what: Driving the widget from the server
title: Using vellumwidget in Shiny
text: Reading interactions one direction; server changing widget shows. vellumwidget_proxy() gives handle widget already page drives without re-rendering — SVG rebuilt, current pan/zoom smooth feel preserved. works exactly like leaflet::leafletProxy(), DT::dataTableProxy(), plotly::plotlyProxy(). Call outputId gave vellumwidgetOutput(), pipe handle verbs: keys element data_ids — identifiers get back input$plot_selected. example, selectInput highlights cars plot, button clears everything: server-driven vw_select() behaves just like user click: projects across mark’s hover_group, propagates linked / crosstalk widgets, updates input$plot_selected. also observe input$plot_selected call vw_select() , guard echo (e.g. act value actually differs) avoid feedback loop — usual Shiny proxy caution.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   selectInput("pick", "Highlight", choices = rownames(mtcars), multiple = TRUE),   actionButton("reset", "Reset"),   vellumwidgetOutput("plot", height = "500px") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$plot <- renderVellumwidget({     vplot(mtcars) |>       mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = rownames(mtcars)) |>       as_widget()   })    # server -> client: highlight and zoom to the picked cars, no re-render   observeEvent(input$pick, ignoreNULL = FALSE, {     vellumwidget_proxy("plot") |>       vw_select(input$pick) |>       vw_zoom(input$pick) # empty selection resets the view   })    observeEvent(input$reset, {     vellumwidget_proxy("plot") |>       vw_clear_selection() |>       vw_reset_zoom()   }) }

title: Using vellumwidget in Shiny
text: data_id (mark key) everything reports; without marks drawn carry identity report. tooltip independent; hover box shows, input carries. inputs fire inside live Shiny session. static render (knitr, pkgdown, htmltools::save_html()) byte--byte identical emits nothing. keys crosstalk SharedData uses, vellumwidget widget can link DT, plotly, leaflet client-side without Shiny. See as_widget(crosstalk=). Shiny inputs crosstalk complementary: use Shiny server needs react, crosstalk pure client-side linking. Driving widget server — selection, filter, zoom — vellumwidget_proxy(), . Shiny inputs (client → server), crosstalk (client ↔︎ client), proxy (server → client) three linking directions. See interactive widgets tour full set client-side interactions, accessibility article keyboard screen-reader model.

title: Authors and Citation
text: David Schoch. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Schoch D (2026). vellumwidget: Interactive Web Widgets Vellum Scenes. R package version 0.6.0.9000, https://r-vellum.github.io/vellumwidget/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {vellumwidget: Interactive Web Widgets for Vellum Scenes},   author = {David Schoch},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.6.0.9000},   url = {https://r-vellum.github.io/vellumwidget/}, }

what: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: vellumwidget turns vellum scene — vellumplot plot — self-contained, client-side interactive HTML widget: hover tooltips + highlighting, click selection, rectangular brush-select, pan/zoom, toolbar, Shiny server round-trip. host adapter vellum interactivity stack: vellum emits per-element data-keys, bounding boxes, scene_model() element table, vellumplot declares interactive, vellumwidget hosts . Interactions: hover (tooltip + highlight, nearest-mark snapping hover_group linking) · shared/unified hover optional crosshair (hover_mode = "x"/"y": one tooltip listing every series hovered x/y) · click-select (single/multiple) · drag rectangle brush-select freehand lasso · wheel / pan-drag pan-zoom · discrete-legend interaction (hover swatch highlight series, click select — legend_click = "hide" toggle/isolate series visibility) · continuous colorbar filter (drag value range colorbar fade --range marks) · optional overview navigator (navigator = TRUE: draggable range strip long series) · toolbar (mode toggle, zoom--selection, reset, save SVG/PNG, fullscreen) · linked views across group (selection, hover, pan/zoom) · keyboard + screen-reader access (Tab , arrow keys move marks, Enter/Space select; a11y, default). opt-outable via as_widget() argument.

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: vellumwidget builds vellum backend (works vellumplot plots); pak pulls automatically. vellum compiles Rust crate, Rust toolchain (cargo/rustc) needed build .
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("r-vellum/vellumwidget")

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: as_widget() terminal: compiles plot vellum scene, emits SVG element table, returns htmlwidget. also accepts bare vellum scene. Declare interactivity vellumplot reserved mark arguments data_id (join key), tooltip, hover_group (links elements shared highlighting); discrete color/shape legends become interactive automatically. plot declares none renders static — still embeddable — SVG.
code: library(vellumplot) library(vellumwidget)  df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget()

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: vellumwidget depends vellum’s scene_svg() / scene_model() contract, wraps vellum scene, whoever produced . vellumplot Suggests (examples/tests).
code: vellumwidget ──depends──▶ vellum ◀──depends── vellumplot

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: JS runtime TypeScript srcts/, bundled esbuild committed inst/htmlwidgets/vellumwidget.js (R package installs Node):
code: npm install            # esbuild + typescript (+ jsdom for tests) npm run build          # srcts/index.ts -> inst/htmlwidgets/vellumwidget.js node tests/js/behavior.test.js   # headless DOM behaviour suite

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: Hover/selection appearance customisable two levels compose — widget-wide theme per-element grammar colours. accept R CSS colour; per-element colours win theme, wins built-defaults. 1. Widget theme — as_widget() arguments set look whole plot: 2. Per-element (data-driven) grammar — declare hover_color / selected_color mark, just like tooltip/data_id. can constants mapped column, different marks highlight differently: layers use CSS-variable mechanism, plot can set theme default override per element pipe. Anything set falls back built-look. (can also override raw CSS classes — .vellumwidget-hl, [data-key].vellumwidget-selected, .vellumwidget-tip, … — host document, arguments supported API.)
code: library(vellumplot) library(vellumwidget)  df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget(     hover_color    = "steelblue",  # outline on hover (default: none, just dim-others)     selected_color = "orange",     # outline on click-select     dim_opacity    = 0.15          # how much the non-hovered marks fade (default 0.28)   ) df$cyl <- factor(mtcars$cyl)  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(     x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model,     hover_color    = ifelse(mtcars$cyl == 8, "firebrick", "steelblue"),     selected_color = "black"   ) |>   as_widget()          # a per-element colour overrides any widget theme

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: Selection can linked across views data key — select brush one plot data highlight everywhere. bus (vellumwidget ↔︎ vellumwidget, dependency). Give widgets shared group: Selection projects field: marks declare hover_group, clicking one element selects whole series (select one cylinder count → cars). crosstalk (interop plotly, leaflet, DT, filter_* inputs). Wrap data crosstalk SharedData (key must match data_id) pass as_widget(): vellumwidget uses selection engine layers crosstalk top optional bridge (SelectionHandle + FilterHandle), crosstalk filter hides non-matching marks (display-tier cross-filter) selection round-trips widgets. crosstalk client library pulled pass SharedData.
code: df$hp <- mtcars$hp  p1 <- vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |> as_widget(group = "cars") p2 <- vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = hp, y = qsec, data_id = model) |> as_widget(group = "cars") # in an HTML doc, selecting a point in p1 highlights the same car in p2 library(crosstalk) sd <- SharedData$new(df, key = ~model, group = "cars")  bscols(   vplot(sd$origData()) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |> as_widget(crosstalk = sd),   DT::datatable(sd)      # selecting rows / points links both ways ) filter_slider("hp", "Horsepower", sd, ~hp)   # crosstalk's filter inputs hide non-matching marks

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: plot maps discrete color shape scale declares interactivity (e.g. data_id), legend swatch becomes handle whole data series — extra arguments needed: vellumplot tags swatch series drives mark series membership; vellumwidget projects swatch event onto every mark series, reusing highlight/select machinery hover_group. Selecting via swatch also links across views crosstalk, exactly like selecting mark.
code: df <- data.frame(   wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg,   model = rownames(mtcars), cyl = factor(mtcars$cyl) ) vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = cyl, data_id = model) |>   as_widget() # hover the "6" swatch -> all six-cylinder cars highlight (the swatch stays lit); # click it -> the whole series is selected (respecting single/multiple mode).

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: vellumwidget interactivity layer small ecosystem packages share vellum scene model: vellum — parchment: low-level graphics backend (Rust scene graph, PNG/SVG/PDF renderer). vellumplot — pen: pipe-first grammar graphics compiles plot spec vellum scene. vellumwidget — annotation: package. vellumverse — installs loads whole ecosystem one step.

what: Turn a vellum scene (or vellumplot plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
title: Turn a vellum scene (or vellumplot plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
text: as_widget() terminal verb interactivity pipeline: compiles input vellum scene, emits SVG (per-element data-keys) vellum::scene_model() element table, bundles vellumwidget JavaScript runtime self-contained htmlwidgets::createWidget() widget. result hover tooltips, hover highlighting, click selection entirely client-side — Shiny, server round-trip. Pan/zoom, brush, selection work mouse, touch (drag pan, two-finger pinch zoom), keyboard input: arrows pan +/-/0 zoom reset, except accessibility (default) arrows move marks one focused — see a11y argument.

title: Turn a vellum scene (or vellumplot plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
code: as_widget(   x,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   tooltip = TRUE,   hover = TRUE,   select = TRUE,   brush = TRUE,   lasso = TRUE,   zoom = TRUE,   toolbar = TRUE,   nearest = TRUE,   navigator = FALSE,   navigator_height = NULL,   axis_zoom = TRUE,   zoom_marks = c("fixed", "scale"),   hover_mode = c("closest", "x", "y"),   crosshair = FALSE,   legend_click = c("select", "hide", "mute"),   a11y = TRUE,   alt = NULL,   hover_color = NULL,   selected_color = NULL,   dim_opacity = NULL,   tooltip_delay = 0,   tooltip_follow = TRUE,   tooltip_sticky = FALSE,   tooltip_style = NULL,   export_filename = NULL,   export_scale = NULL,   group = NULL,   crosstalk = NULL,   select_mode = c("multiple", "single"),   mode = c("auto", "svg", "raster"),   raster_threshold = 20000L,   text = c("native", "outline"),   elementId = NULL )

title: Turn a vellum scene (or vellumplot plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
text:
x vellumplot plot (PlotSpec / PlotComposition) vellum scene — anything vellum::as_vellum_scene() accepts. width, height Widget size (valid CSS size, NULL size scene). Passed htmlwidgets::createWidget(). tooltip, hover, select Toggles three hover/click interactions (TRUE). brush, zoom, toolbar Toggles rectangular brush-select, wheel/drag pan-zoom (via SVG viewBox), -hover toolbar (TRUE). lasso Enable freehand lasso-select (default TRUE): third drag mode alongside brush pan, cycled toolbar's mode button. Drag loop every mark whose centre falls inside selected. Like brush, reports input$_brush (lasso = TRUE flag loop's bounding box). mode button appears whenever least two drag modes enabled. nearest TRUE (default), hover snaps nearest mark within small radius cursor directly one (helps sparse points). navigator Show overview range navigator plot (default FALSE): full-width strip rendering whole scene miniature, draggable, resizable window marking visible x-range. Drag window pan, drag handle zoom; stays two-way sync main view (wheel/keyboard/brush linked-group changes move ). Useful long series. navigator_height sets strip height pixels (default 56). navigator_height Height navigator strip pixels (default 56); ignored unless navigator = TRUE. axis_zoom Axis-aware zoom (default TRUE). Wheel/drag zoom scales plot's data region re-ticks axes visible range, holding frame — axes, titles legend — place, way chart library zooms (rather scaling whole scene like image). Applies single linear cartesian panel (continuous identity/reverse axes) rendered SVG; plots log/date/discrete axes, several panels, raster mode silently fall back ordinary whole-scene zoom, leaving always safe. Set FALSE force plain whole-scene viewBox zoom. Builds vellum's pannable-panel contract (vl_viewport(pannable=)) panel scale metadata vellumplot emits (needs current vellum/vellumplot). combined navigator = TRUE, navigator's x-zoom rendered , x-axis re-ticks crisply instead stretching. zoom_marks axis-aware zoom, whether glyph marks (points, circles, hexagons, sector wedges) keep constant pixel size. "fixed" (default): glyphs stay original size positions re-map, way charting library zooms — points stay round (stretch ellipses navigator's x-zoom). "scale": glyphs scale zoom (older behaviour; useful read density). Positional marks (bars, error bars, lines, areas) always scale data either way; stroke width held constant. effect unless axis_zoom active. hover_mode hover gathers marks tooltip. "closest" (default) shows single nearest mark. "x" ("y") gives unified hover: every mark sharing hovered x (y) position highlighted listed together one box — shared readout multi-series line time-series charts expect. Unified mode always snaps along axis, readout tracks cursor regardless nearest. mark positions come element index, axis metadata required; box lists mark's tooltip (one row per series) without value-axis header. crosshair Draw guide rule hovered position (default FALSE): vertical rule shared x hover_mode = "x", horizontal rule "y", full cross mark "closest". Colour --vellumwidget-crosshair-stroke CSS variable (muted grey default). legend_click clicking discrete-legend swatch . "select" (default) selects swatch's whole series (established behaviour). "hide" makes legend visibility toggle — single click hides/shows series, double-click isolates (hides every series; double-click restore ) — reflexive legend interaction plotly / ECharts / Highcharts. "mute" dims series instead removing (layout kept). Hovering swatch still highlights series every policy. Applies plot draws interactive legend (discrete color / shape scale vellumplot). a11y Accessibility (default TRUE). Makes widget keyboard- screen-reader-navigable chart: SVG labelled interactive chart (role="graphics-document"), mark focusable graphics-symbol roving tabindex (arrow keys move marks, Enter/Space select, Escape exits), polite aria-live region announces focused/selected mark, visually-hidden data table lists every mark assistive tech. FALSE restores previous behaviour (chart semantics, marks focusable). alt Accessible label (alt text) chart whole. Defaults scene's title/description — vellumplot sets automatically plot title vellumplot::plot_alt() — explicit value needed raw vellum scene override. hover_color, selected_color Outline colours hovered / selected elements (R CSS colour), applied widget-wide. hover_color = NULL (default) keeps plain dim-others hover; selected_color = NULL uses built-default. per-mark hover_color/selected_color declared vellumplot overrides mark. dim_opacity Opacity (0–1) non-hovered elements hovering (default 0.28); NULL keeps default. tooltip_delay Milliseconds wait tooltip appears hover (default 0, .e. immediate). highlight unaffected — tooltip waits. short delay (e.g. 250) calms dense scatter. tooltip_follow TRUE (default) tooltip tracks cursor; FALSE anchors hovered mark's centre. tooltip_sticky TRUE, tooltip accepts pointer events lingers briefly leave mark, can move — tooltips contain links buttons (build HTML via tooltip =). Default FALSE. tooltip_style Optional named list styling tooltip box: background / color (R CSS colour), fontsize, max_width (CSS length). NULL (default) uses built-style. Tooltip text rendered safe HTML — author-built tooltip = (e.g. via glue()) may use /<>/
bold/italic/line breaks; data values escaped inert tags honoured (scripts/attributes). export_filename, export_scale download filename base (extension; default "plot") PNG resolution multiplier (default 1) toolbar's SVG/PNG export. Exports capture current (zoomed/panned) view. group Optional linking group name. Widgets sharing group link client-side: selecting (brushing) one highlights data keys others, panning/zooming one pans/zooms others relative view — Shiny, crosstalk. Selection projects hover_group marks declare (select one, select whole series). Linked pan/zoom shares view fraction widget's extent, links correctly even across differently-sized plots (e.g. small multiples). crosstalk Optional crosstalk::SharedData (crosstalk group name string) link widget crosstalk ecosystem (plotly, leaflet, DT, crosstalk's filter_* inputs). widget's data_ids must match SharedData's keys. crosstalk filter hides non-matching elements (display-tier cross-filter). Requires crosstalk package. select_mode "multiple" (default; click toggles element) "single" (click replaces selection). mode Rendering strategy marks. "auto" (default) ships per-element SVG small/moderate plots switches single embedded raster image raster_threshold keyed elements; "svg" always uses per-element SVG; "raster" always uses image. raster mode marks drawn base image interaction (hover, click, brush, pan/zoom) driven client-side element index (bounding boxes + keys), large scatter (100k+ points) stays navigable tiny DOM small payload. trade-offs raster mode: per-element grammar colours, per-mark screen-reader focus, display-tier cross-filtering apply (per-element DOM nodes), zoomed-view scaled raster re-rendered. raster_threshold Keyed-element count mode = "auto" switches raster image (default 20000). text text written SVG, passed vellum::scene_svg(): "native" (default) emits selectable referencing system fonts — smaller page font, post-processable, better accessibility LLMs; "outline" emits glyph outlines pixel-faithful font-independent selectable. Applies per-element SVG path ; raster mode (see mode) text baked base image argument ignored (warning set explicitly). elementId Optional explicit widget DOM id.

title: Turn a vellum scene (or vellumplot plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
text: htmlwidget class "vellumwidget".

title: Turn a vellum scene (or vellumplot plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
text: Interactivity driven keys/metadata plot declares. vellumplot come reserved data_id / tooltip / hover_group mark arguments; plot declares none renders static (still embeddable) SVG. hovered element data_id tooltip shows key. scene metadata vellumwidget reads — vellum::scene_model() element table SVG data-key / data-vellum-* attributes — specified vellum's "scene contract" vignette (vignette("scene-contract", package = "vellum")).

title: Turn a vellum scene (or vellumplot plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget

what: vellumwidget: Interactive Web Widgets for Vellum Scenes — vellumwidget-package

text: Turns 'vellum' scene ('vellumplot' plot) self-contained, client-side interactive HTML widget: hover tooltips highlighting, click selection, rectangular brush-select, pan/zoom, toolbar – driven per-element data keys, bounding boxes, 'scene_model()' metadata 'vellum' emits. Requires Shiny server round-trip.

title: Proxy verbs: drive a rendered widget from the server — vellumwidget-proxy-verbs

title: Proxy verbs: drive a rendered widget from the server — vellumwidget-proxy-verbs
text: proxy vellumwidget_proxy(). keys Character vector element data keys (data_ids vellumplot mark declares). vw_select()/vw_filter() empty vector meaningful (select nothing / hide everything); use vw_clear_selection() / vw_clear_filter() remove selection filter. vw_zoom() empty vector resets full view.

title: Proxy verbs: drive a rendered widget from the server — vellumwidget-proxy-verbs

title: Shiny bindings for vellumwidget widgets — vellumwidget-shiny
code: vellumwidgetOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  renderVellumwidget(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for vellumwidget widgets — vellumwidget-shiny
text: outputId Shiny output slot id. width, height Widget size. expr expression producing vellumwidget widget. env, quoted Standard non-standard-evaluation plumbing.

title: Shiny bindings for vellumwidget widgets — vellumwidget-shiny
text: vellumwidgetOutput(): Shiny output UI element. renderVellumwidget(): Shiny render function.

title: Shiny bindings for vellumwidget widgets — vellumwidget-shiny
text: widget rendered vellumwidgetOutput("plot") reports user's interactions back server reactive inputs, keyed output id. values element data keys (data_id vellumplot mark declares); map back data key. input$plot_selected Character vector currently selected keys (click / brush / keyboard, selection arriving linked widget). Updates state — re-selecting set -op. character(0) nothing selected. input$plot_click list list(key=) click; key NULL click empty space. event input — fires every click, even mark twice. input$plot_hover hovered key, NULL pointer leaves mark. Updates state (re-fires hovered key changes). input$plot_brush list list(keys=, x0=, y0=, x1=, y1=) brush (lasso) gesture completes: selected keys region's bounding rectangle scene's device-pixel (viewBox) coordinates. lasso gesture also carries lasso = TRUE. plot carries cartesian scale (vellumplot plot), also carries region's data-space bounds x0d,y0d,x1d,y1d panel name. event input. input$plot_colorfilter length-2 numeric c(lo, hi) — value range selected continuous colorbar (see interactive colorbar filter); NULL full range shown. State input. input$plot_zoom list list(x=, y=, w=, h=, zoomed=) — current view (SVG viewBox device-pixel coordinates) plus zoomed flag (view narrower/shorter full extent). single-panel cartesian plot also carries data = list(x=c(lo,hi), y=c(lo,hi), panel=), visible range data coordinates. Updates state zoom/pan settles (wheel, drag-pan release, pinch, keyboard, reset, zoom--selection, proxy vw_zoom()). Data-space coordinates (x0d/… zoom$data) come per-panel scale descriptors vellumplot attaches scene; raw vellum scene non-cartesian coordinate system carries none, device-pixel fields reported. specifics: describe visual axes: x0d/x1d (zoom$data$x) horizontal axis. coord_flip() plot's y aesthetic. Date/time axes report numeric epoch (days Date, seconds POSIXct); map back .Date() / .POSIXct(). discrete axis reports fractional band positions (category sits ), level labels. Axes built custom scales::transform_*() object (anything beyond identity / log10 / sqrt / reverse) inverted client-side, axis omitted data-space fields (device-pixel fields still report). emitted inside live Shiny session; static render (knitr, pkgdown, htmltools::save_html()) produces identical output input traffic. drive widget server — set selection, cross-filter , zoom without re-render — use vellumwidget_proxy().

what: Drive an already-rendered widget from the Shiny server — vellumwidget_proxy
title: Drive an already-rendered widget from the Shiny server — vellumwidget_proxy
text: vellumwidget_proxy() server--client counterpart input read-back documented vellumwidget-shiny. returns lightweight handle widget already page, server can change widget shows — set selection, cross-filter , zoom — without re-rendering (renderVellumwidget() round-trip, full redraw, lost pan/zoom). mirrors leaflet::leafletProxy() / DT::dataTableProxy() / plotly::plotlyProxy().

title: Drive an already-rendered widget from the Shiny server — vellumwidget_proxy
code: vellumwidget_proxy(outputId, session = NULL)

title: Drive an already-rendered widget from the Shiny server — vellumwidget_proxy
text: outputId id vellumwidgetOutput() control (un-namespaced id, passed vellumwidgetOutput(); module namespacing handled ). session Shiny session; defaults current reactive domain, rarely pass explicitly.

title: Drive an already-rendered widget from the Shiny server — vellumwidget_proxy
text: object class "vellumwidget_proxy" (invisibly verbs), piped vw_select() proxy verbs.

title: Drive an already-rendered widget from the Shiny server — vellumwidget_proxy
text: Call inside observe() / observeEvent() outputId gave vellumwidgetOutput(), pipe handle one verbs . verb sends single custom message browser returns proxy invisibly, calls chain pipe. vw_select() Replace widget's selection keys (element data_ids). Selecting projects across mark's hover_group propagates linked / crosstalk widgets, exactly user click , updates input$<id>_selected. vw_clear_selection() Clear selection. vw_filter() Cross-filter: show elements whose key keys dim/hide rest (display tier — data untouched). vw_clear_filter() Remove filter (show everything). vw_zoom() Zoom/pan view frame elements keys. vw_reset_zoom() Restore original (full) view. keys element data keys — data_id vellumplot mark declares — identifiers receive back input$<id>_selected friends.

title: Drive an already-rendered widget from the Shiny server — vellumwidget_proxy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(shiny) server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$plot <- renderVellumwidget(my_widget)   # A server-side control drives the plot without redrawing it:   observeEvent(input$highlight, {     vellumwidget_proxy("plot") |> vw_select(input$highlight)   })   observeEvent(input$reset, {     vellumwidget_proxy("plot") |> vw_clear_selection() |> vw_reset_zoom()   }) } } # }

title: vellumwidget (development version)
text: Declarative interactivity: cross-view filtering. selection defined one cell composition referenced filter_by() another now cross-filters — brush/select source cell linked cell narrows matching rows, matched cross-view join id. source cell stays fully visible; target cell hides (scoped per-cell-unique keys). one widget runtime, server round-trip. Declarative interactivity: filters. vellumplot plot declares filter_by(selection) now hides everything outside selection selection’s gesture fires — reusing cross-filter path. Driven entirely plot spec (as_widget() argument). Single-view now; cross-view form (brush one panel, filter another) follows. Declarative interactivity: conditional encodings. as_widget() now reads interaction model vellumplot plot declares (select_point() / condition()) compiled spec enacts frozen scene. hover- click-driven selection condition() aesthetic keeps selection members if_true style applies if_false non-members (explicit colour, theme dim spotlight) — reverting selection empties. new as_widget() argument: behaviour travels plot. (Filters, cross-view, scale binds follow.)

title: vellumwidget 0.6.0
text: Bug fix: legend swatches longer jump -screen axis-aware zoom. axis_zoom/zoom_marks = "fixed" (default), constant-size counter- scale applied every glyph mark, including discrete legend swatches. swatch positioned transform attribute (unlike data points, carry absolute coordinates), inline counter-scale overrode attribute flung swatch corner. counter-scale now restricted marks inside pannable data region, legend swatches stay put. Axis-aware zoom (axis_zoom, default TRUE). Wheel/drag zoom scales plot’s data region re-ticks axes visible range — holding frame (axes, titles, legend) place way charting library zooms, rather scaling whole scene like image. Hit-testing (hover/brush/lasso), crosshair, input$<id>_zoom data range follow zoomed data region. Applies single linear cartesian panel (continuous identity/reverse axes) rendered SVG; plots log/date/discrete axes, several panels, raster mode silently fall back ordinary whole-scene zoom, safe leave . Set axis_zoom = FALSE plain whole-scene zoom. Built vellum’s pannable-panel contract (vl_viewport(pannable=)) panel scale metadata vellumplot emits (needs current development vellum/vellumplot). Constant-size markers zoom (zoom_marks, default "fixed"). axis-aware zoom, glyph marks (points, circles, hexagons, sector wedges) now keep original pixel size positions re-map — points stay round don’t stretch ellipses navigator’s x-zoom, way charting library zooms. Positional marks (bars, error bars, lines, areas) still scale data; stroke width held constant. Set zoom_marks = "scale" old behaviour glyphs grow zoom (useful read density). Applies SVG axis-aware zoom raster crisp-point layer. Interactive continuous colorbar filter (visualMap). vellumplot plot maps continuous color scale, colorbar becomes range filter: drag two handles gradient bar value range marks whose colour value falls outside fade (drop hover/brush hit-testing); double-click bar reset. selected range reported Shiny input$<id>_colorfilter = c(lo, hi) (NULL full range). Automatic — argument set — whenever plot continuous colorbar (needs current development vellumplot; SVG mode). Discrete/binned colour legends keep existing click--hide interaction. Tooltip polish. as_widget() gains tooltip_delay (ms wait tooltip appears — highlight immediate, tooltip waits), tooltip_follow (TRUE, default, tracks cursor; FALSE anchors mark), tooltip_sticky (tooltip accepts pointer events lingers briefly leave, tooltips containing links/buttons usable). tooltip also now auto-flips cursor isn’t room clamps horizontally doesn’t overflow widget. Overview navigator (navigator = TRUE). opt-strip plot renders whole scene miniature draggable, resizable window marking visible x-range: drag window pan, drag handle zoom. Zoom x-— selected x-range fills width full y-range stays screen (time-series range selector). axis_zoom (default) rendered axis-aware zoom, x-axis re-ticks crisply; otherwise view stretches horizontally. stays two-way sync main view — wheel/keyboard/brush, toolbar, linked-group pan/zoom move window, moving window drives . Useful scrubbing long series. navigator_height sets strip height (default 56px). Client-side; default. Linked pan/zoom across group. Widgets sharing group already linked selection hover; now panning zooming one moves others view. view shared fraction widget’s extent (client-side bus, Shiny/crosstalk), linked plots different sizes — small multiples — stay aligned. Reset links . Brush view now report data-space coordinates, just pixels. plot carries cartesian scale (vellumplot plot), input$<id>_brush gains brushed region’s data-space bounds x0d,y0d,x1d,y1d (plus panel name) alongside existing device-pixel rectangle, input$<id>_zoom gains data = list(x=, y=, panel=), visible range data coordinates. reads per-panel scale descriptors vellumplot now attaches scene (requires current development vellum (>= 0.4.0.9000) vellumplot); raw vellum scene non-cartesian coordinate system reports device-pixel fields , . Date/time axes report numeric epoch (days Date, seconds POSIXct), map back .Date() / .POSIXct(). fields describe visual axes (coord_flip(), x0d plot’s y aesthetic); discrete axis reports fractional band positions; axis built custom scales::transform_*() object (beyond identity / log10 / sqrt / reverse) omitted data-space fields rather reported wrong. (Groundwork axis-aware zoom.) Fix: cross-filtered legend-hidden marks still hit-testable. display-tier cross-filter (crosstalk vw_filter()) legend legend_click = "hide" toggle set marks display:none, stayed spatial index — nearest-mark hover still tooltip hidden datum, brush lasso re-select filtered-points (propagating selection back linked/crosstalk views). Keyboard traversal already skipped hidden marks; hover, brush, lasso, raster click-snap now , via single “inert” guard. Muted (hidden) legend series stay interactive. Freehand lasso-select. third drag mode alongside brush pan (default ; disable as_widget(lasso = FALSE)). toolbar’s mode button now cycles brush → lasso → pan, appears whenever least two drag modes enabled. Drag loop every mark whose centre falls inside selected — hit-tested point--polygon check Flatbush spatial index, stays fast large scenes. reports input$<id>_brush like box brush, lasso = TRUE flag loop’s bounding box. Widgets report current view Shiny (input$<id>_zoom). zoom/pan settles — wheel, drag-pan release, pinch, keyboard, reset, zoom--selection, vw_zoom() proxy call — widget publishes list(x=, y=, w=, h=, zoomed=): current viewBox (device-px) whether view zoomed . deduped state input, coordinated dashboard can react user looking . (Data-space limits await axis/scale metadata scene contract.) Legend click--hide double-click--isolate. as_widget() gains legend_click argument. default "select" unchanged (clicking discrete-legend swatch selects series). "hide" turns legend visibility toggle — single click hides shows series, double-click isolates (hides every series; double-click restore ) — reflexive legend interaction plotly / ECharts / Highcharts. "mute" dims series rather removing (keeping layout). Hovering swatch still highlights series every policy, hidden series drop hover/tooltip hit-testing. Works keyboard (Enter/Space focused swatch toggles) independent crosstalk cross-filter (two never clobber ). Entirely client-side. Shared (unified) hover tooltips crosshair. as_widget() gains hover_mode argument. default "closest" unchanged (hover shows single nearest mark), "x" ("y") turns unified hover: every mark sharing hovered x (y) position highlighted listed together one tooltip box — shared readout multi-series line time-series charts expect, want every series’ value cursor’s x rather one point. companion crosshair argument (default FALSE) draws guide rule hovered position: vertical rule shared x "x" mode, horizontal one "y" mode, full cross mark "closest" mode. entirely client-side need extra scene metadata — mark positions come straight element index — work keyed vellumplot plot raw vellum scene, raster mode. Unified hover snaps along axis readout tracks cursor anywhere plot; hover Shiny input still reports single nearest (primary) mark, read-back contract unchanged. Fix: hover/selection feedback rings drawn offset mark. widget container larger plot’s rendered box — happens routinely, htmlwidgets stamps explicit height container fluid layout can stretch width — feedback overlay filled whole container base SVG filled aspect-locked box. overlay’s viewBox letterboxed (centred), every hover/selection ring drew real mark: downward container taller (e.g. 68px low 520px-tall container 384px plot) sideways wider. looked like wrong mark picked. Hit-testing always correct; visual feedback displaced. svg overlays now share shrink--fit stage, overlay tracks svg box exactly container size.

title: vellumwidget 0.5.0
text: text argument as_widget(). Choose text written SVG, passed vellum::scene_svg(): "native" (default) emits selectable <text> referencing system fonts — smaller page font, post-processable, better accessibility LLMs — "outline" emits pixel-faithful, font-independent glyph paths. Applies per-element SVG path ; raster mode text baked base image argument ignored (warning set explicitly) (#1). New articles. Two articles document features added since 0.4.0: Linking views crosstalk (coordinating widget DT / plotly / leaflet crosstalk’s filter_* inputs) large scenes (raster mode, spatial index, columnar payload, crisp zoom). Crisp zoom raster mode. zoom raster-mode plot, base image used upscale blur. widget now redraws points sharply <canvas> overlay zoomed — sampling point’s colour straight rendered image position/size element index, crisp layer matches vellum drew. engages zoomed (faithful, anti-aliased base image still shows full view), redraws just points view, degrades gracefully image alone 2D canvas context isn’t available. Entirely client-side; change payload small/moderate (SVG-mode) plots. large scatterplots navigable (raster mode). as_widget() gains mode argument ("auto" / "svg" / "raster"). "auto" (default), scene raster_threshold keyed elements (default 20000) drawn single embedded image instead one SVG node per element, interaction — hover tooltips + highlight, click/brush select, pan/zoom — driven client-side element index (bounding boxes + keys) rather DOM. 150k-point keyed scatter previously produced ~75 MB SVG 150,000 DOM nodes now ships ~0.8 MB image plus compact index, handful DOM nodes, stays smooth hover pan. Small moderate plots unchanged (keep per-element SVG). Trade-offs raster mode: per-element grammar colours, per-mark screen-reader focus, display-tier cross-filtering don’t apply (per-element nodes), zoomed-view scaled raster re-rendered. Adds dependency base64enc. Smoother hover, brush, pan large plots. Two client-side changes lift per-interaction cost made big scatterplots laggy: Spatial index. Nearest-mark hover rectangular brush now hit-test Flatbush R-tree (O(log n) / O(k)) instead scanning every element time. nearest-mark scan runs every pointer move, change feel . Cheaper hover dim. threshold, hovering dims plot (via holder’s opacity) re-draws hovered marks crisply small overlay — O(hovered) — instead restyling every element via CSS (O(n)), forced full-scene style recalc hover. Small moderate plots keep exact previous per-mark dim. (Phase 2 vellum’s big-data interactivity plan.) Much faster, smaller payload large plots (columnar element table). keyed-element metadata as_widget() embeds now serialised columnar form (one array per field) instead one JSON object per element. transparent wire-format change — widget behaves identically — removes per-element serialisation cost dominated large N. 150,000-point keyed scatter payload build + serialise dropped ~24 s / 89 MB ~0.4 s / 12 MB (~60x faster), big interactive scatter longer choked payload generation. Small moderate plots unaffected. (Phase 1 vellum’s big-data interactivity plan; browser-side DOM/hover work truly huge scatters follows later phases.) Error bars boxplots interactive. Now vellumplot keys statistical marks, widget hovers, tooltips, clicks/selects, brushes units: error bar’s bar + caps, box’s rect + median + whiskers, light select together share one data-key (outliers stay individually addressable). Brush selection longer double-counts mark whose key spans several SVG elements — input$<id>_brush$keys reports key . runtime change needed core behaviour; already grouped every node sharing key.

title: vellumwidget 0.4.0
text: vw_select() / vw_clear_selection() — set clear selection (projects across hover_group propagates linked / crosstalk widgets, exactly like user click, updates input$<id>_selected); vw_filter() / vw_clear_filter() — cross-filter widget (show given keys, dim rest; display tier, data untouched); vw_zoom() / vw_reset_zoom() — frame set keys, restore full view.

title: vellumwidget 0.3.0
text: Adopted vellum’s renamed vl_* graphics primitives (grid collision fix). Shiny input read-back. widget rendered vellumwidgetOutput() now reports user’s interactions server reactive inputs keyed output id: input$<id>_selected (selected keys, state), input$<id>_click (list(key=), event), input$<id>_hover (hovered key NULL, state), input$<id>_brush (list(keys=, x0=, y0=, x1=, y1=), event). Values element data keys, map straight back data. Emitted live Shiny session — static render unchanged produces input traffic. See new Using vellumwidget Shiny article. (Driving widget server — vellumwidget_proxy() — planned follow-.) Accessibility (a11y = TRUE, default). interactive widget now keyboard- screen-reader-navigable, mute image: SVG announced interactive chart (role="graphics-document" + aria-roledescription), labelled scene’s title/description (vellumplot sets automatically) explicit as_widget(alt =); every mark focusable graphics-symbol roving tabindex — arrow keys move marks, Enter/Space toggles selection, Escape exits; polite aria-live region announces focused / selected mark; visually-hidden data table lists every mark assistive tech. gated a11y; a11y = FALSE restores previous output exactly. See vellumplot Accessibility article.

title: vellumwidget 0.2.0
text:
Interaction-depth release (ROADMAP §4). Rich tooltips. Tooltip text now renders safe HTML: author-built tooltip = (e.g. via glue()) may use /<>/
bold/italic/ line breaks. Data values escaped inert, attribute-free tags honoured — scripts, handlers, attributes (XSS). New as_widget(tooltip_style =) themes tooltip box (background/color/fontsize/ max_width). Touch + keyboard. widget driven pointer events, pan/brush/ hover work mouse, touch, pen one path; two-finger pinch zooms. widget focused, arrow keys pan, +/- zoom, 0 resets. Configurable export. as_widget(export_filename =, export_scale =) set download filename base hi-res PNG multiplier; exports capture current (zoomed/panned) view. “copy PNG clipboard” toolbar button appears Clipboard API available. Large-N performance. Hover/selection highlighting uses per-render key→node cache instead querySelectorAll per key, nearest-mark hover scan throttled one per animation frame. New “Interactive widgets: tour” article. Deferred: spatial index hit-testing (DOM cost real bottleneck, now cached; bbox scans cheap datashade collapses huge clouds raster); coordinated zoom across linked widgets URL deep-linking; PDF export.

title: vellumwidget 0.1.0
text: First release. vellumwidget turns vellum scene vellumplot plot self-contained, client-side interactive HTML widget via single terminal as_widget() pipe — Shiny, server round-trip. Everything ships first release.

title: vellumwidget 0.1.0
text: as_widget() compiles vellumplot plot raw vellum scene bundles SVG + scene_model() element table htmlwidget. Hover — tooltip (declared tooltip, falling back key) highlight inverse-dim; hovered element’s hover_group highlights whole group. Hover snaps nearest mark cursor isn’t directly one. Click — select (single / multiple modes); every element sharing key toggles together. Brush — drag rectangle select every element covers. Pan / zoom — mouse wheel pan-mode drag reframe SVG viewBox, plus zoom--selection reset. Toolbar (hover) — brush/pan mode toggle, zoom--selection, reset zoom, download SVG, download PNG, fullscreen. Everything opt-outable via as_widget() arguments (tooltip/hover/select/brush/zoom/toolbar/nearest). Legend interaction. discrete color/shape scale interactive plot, legend swatch drives whole data series: hovering swatch highlights series (swatch stays lit), clicking selects series (links across views / crosstalk). Automatic — extra arguments. Linked views. as_widget(group=) links vellumwidget widgets client-side (dependency): selecting/brushing one highlights data keys others, projecting hover_group (select one, select series). as_widget(crosstalk = SharedData) bridges crosstalk ecosystem (plotly / leaflet / DT filter_* inputs) via SelectionHandle + FilterHandle; crosstalk filter hides non-matching marks (display-tier cross-filter). crosstalk Suggests, loaded used. Widget theme — as_widget(hover_color=, selected_color=, dim_opacity=) sets look whole plot (R CSS colour). Per-element grammar — vellumplot marks’ hover_color/selected_color (constant column-mapped) style element individually override theme. use CSS variables defaults ← theme ← per-element cascade. JS runtime TypeScript srcts/, bundled esbuild committed inst/htmlwidgets/vellumwidget.js (R package installs Node).
